records.ety: [OF. braon fleshy part, muscle, fr. HG. brāto flesh, G. braten roast meat; akin to Icel. br<?/<?/ flesh, food of beasts, AS. br<?/de roast meat, br<?/dan to roast, G. braten, and possibly to E. breed.]

quotes: Formed well of brawns and of bones.

defs: Full, strong muscles, esp. of the arm or leg, muscular strength; a protuberant muscular part of the body; sometimes, the arm.
quotes: It was ordained that murderers should be brent on the brawn of the left hand.
And in my vantbrace put this withered brawn.

defs: The flesh of a boar; also, the salted and prepared flesh of a boar.
quotes: The best age for the boar is from two to five years, at which time it is best to geld him, or sell him for brawn.
